articles.title: Actor Martin Clunes Talks About Playing a Newsreader in a Drama
articles.summary: Martin Clunes, an actor from the show Doc Martin, is getting ready to play Huw Edwards in a new TV drama. Huw Edwards was a famous BBC newsreader who told big news stories for many years. He got into trouble for having bad pictures of children and went to court. Clunes says actors should not judge the people they play. He learned that Edwards was not fun to work with from people who knew him. The drama also shows a young man who was hurt by Edwards. Clunes wants to tell the story of this young man and his family, not just about Edwards. Edwards left the BBC and was sentenced to jail in 2024. Clunes was surprised when he found out it was Edwards in the news.

articles.title: A Supernova That Helps Us Learn About Dark Energy
articles.summary: Astronomers have found a special supernova from 10 billion years ago that might help solve a big mystery about the universe. Dark energy is a force that makes the universe grow faster, but we don't know what it is. This supernova is very bright because a galaxy's gravity bends its light, making it look bigger. The light splits into multiple images, and each image shows the supernova at a slightly different time. By studying these images, scientists can measure how fast the universe is expanding. This could help them understand dark energy better and fix a problem where different measurements of the expansion rate don't match. The discovery is exciting because it uses gravity to see faraway things and might answer big questions about space.

articles.title: A Big Twist in Space's Oldest Light
articles.summary: Scientists are studying a strange twist in the oldest light in the universe, called the cosmic microwave background. This light is leftover from the Big Bang and helps us learn about space. They found that this light might be rotating slightly, which is called cosmic birefringence. This twist could be caused by tiny particles called axions. Researchers measured the twist angle and think it might be bigger than they thought before. They used a new method to make their measurements more accurate. This helps scientists understand dark matter and dark energy better. Future space experiments will use this method to learn more about the universe.

articles.title: Making Train Ticket Claims Easier and Fairer
articles.summary: Train companies are making changes to help passengers. Starting in April, there will be a trial to check railcards more carefully to stop fraud and save money. This will also protect people who make honest mistakes with their tickets. Another change will make it easier for passengers to get money back if their train is late or cancelled. Instead of dealing with the train company, they can ask the place where they bought the ticket, like Trainline. These changes aim to make train travel simpler and fairer for everyone, so passengers feel confident and get the right discounts and compensation.

articles.title: Emma Raducanu Gets Sick and Misses a Tennis Tournament
articles.summary: Emma Raducanu is a famous tennis player from Britain who won a big tournament called the US Open in 2021. She was supposed to play in the Miami Open, a tennis event in Florida from March 17 to 29, but she had to withdraw because she is sick. She got ill last month while playing in the Middle East and still feels unwell with post-viral symptoms. Last week, she played in Indian Wells and reached the third round before losing. She decided it is best to take a break to recover fully. She will try to play again in three weeks at a tournament in Linz. Another British player, Sonay Kartal, also cannot play in Miami because of a back injury. Fran Jones, another British player, will play against Venus Williams, a famous American tennis player, in the first round.

articles.title: Funny TV Show Comes to the UK with Famous Hosts
articles.summary: A new TV show called Saturday Night Live UK is starting in the UK. It is based on a popular American show that makes people laugh with sketches and music. The first three hosts are Tina Fey, Jamie Dornan, and Riz Ahmed. Tina Fey will host the first episode on March 21 with a band called Wet Leg. Jamie Dornan hosts the next week with Wolf Alice, and Riz Ahmed hosts after that with Kasabian. The show will be on Sky TV every Saturday at 10 PM from London. Each episode is 75 minutes long and includes funny sketches, live music, and news jokes. A group of UK comedians will perform in the show, and writers will prepare it each week. The show is live, so people in the studio watch it as it happens.
